summary: WebSocket real-time updates feature fully tested and verified. Backend WebSocket endpoint /api/ws accepts connections and broadcasts order_change messages on all order operations (create, update, move, delete, bulk-move). Frontend useOrders.js establishes WebSocket connection on mount, receives order_change messages, and triggers silent refresh (no loading spinner). Auto-reconnect after disconnect works (3s delay). Real-time updates demonstrated: new orders appear automatically in dashboard without manual refresh.
features_tested: Backend: WebSocket endpoint /api/ws accepts connections - PASS
Backend: WebSocket stays open (tested 3 seconds) - PASS
Backend: Broadcast on order create (POST /api/orders) - PASS
Backend: Broadcast on order update (PUT /api/orders/{id}) - PASS
Backend: Broadcast on order move (POST /api/orders/{id}/move) - PASS
Backend: Broadcast on order delete (DELETE /api/orders/{id}) - PASS
Backend: WebSocket message format is JSON with 'type' and 'data' fields - PASS
Backend: Message includes 'action' field (create, update, move, delete, bulk_move) - PASS
Frontend: useOrders.js establishes WebSocket connection on mount - PASS
Frontend: On receiving 'order_change', orders are silently refreshed - PASS
Frontend: Silent refresh works - no loading spinner appears - PASS
Frontend: Auto-reconnect after disconnect (3s delay) - PASS
Frontend: Dashboard auto-updates when order created via API - PASS

integration_issues.issue: Console shows 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established' occasionally, but auto-reconnect handles it and updates work
integration_issues.notes: This is likely due to preview environment network/proxy issues. The auto-reconnect mechanism successfully recovers.

issues: Hydration warnings in console (span inside tr/tbody) - cosmetic, does not affect functionality - existing issue from previous iterations

tests.test: Real-time update: Order created via API appears in dashboard
tests.details: Order count increased from 1 to 2, new order visible automatically

tests.test: Silent refresh: No loading overlay during update
tests.details: No loading overlay or loading text visible during real-time refresh

critical_code_review_comments: ws_manager.py: ConnectionManager correctly implements connect/disconnect/broadcast with connection tracking
server.py: WebSocket endpoint /api/ws properly handles connection lifecycle with try/except for WebSocketDisconnect
orders.py: All CRUD operations (create, update, move, delete, bulk-move) call ws_manager.broadcast with correct action type
useOrders.js: WS_URL correctly derived from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (http->ws, https->wss)
useOrders.js: connectWs uses useCallback with proper dependencies [fetchOrders, fetchProductionSummary]
useOrders.js: fetchOrders(true) correctly skips setLoading(true) for silent refresh
useOrders.js: Auto-reconnect implemented with 3s setTimeout on ws.onclose
useOrders.js: Cleanup on unmount properly clears timeout and closes WebSocket
seed_data_creation: Created admin test session via MongoDB for API and UI testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: WebSocket real-time updates feature verified working. Backend: ws_manager.py with ConnectionManager, server.py WebSocket endpoint at /api/ws (lines 45-54), orders.py broadcasts on all CRUD operations. Frontend: useOrders.js lines 6-9 WS_URL derivation, lines 134-168 WebSocket connection with auto-reconnect, lines 31-47 fetchOrders with silent parameter. Test session: test_session_ws_1772813196688. Note: Console may show 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established' occasionally but auto-reconnect handles it.
